content.sanskrit: असतो मा सद्गमय तमसो मा ज्योतिर्गमय मृत्योर्मा अमृतं गमय ॥
content.transliteration: asato mā sadgamaya tamaso mā jyotirgamaya mṛtyormā amṛtaṁ gamaya ||
content.text: Lead me from the unreal to the real. Lead me from darkness to light. Lead me from death to immortality.
I am Brahman (Aham Brahmasmi). This is the Mahavakya declaring the unity of the individual self with the universal Brahman.
Not this, not this (Neti Neti). Brahman transcends all limiting attributes — the classical Upanishadic method of approaching the Absolute through negation.
Brahman is pure knowledge, pure bliss — the goal of the giver of gifts, the refuge of one who knows and stands firm.

content.sanskrit: एतस्य वा अक्षरस्य प्रशासने गार्गि सूर्यचन्द्रमसौ विधृतौ तिष्ठतः ।
content.transliteration: etasya vā akṣarasya praśāsane gārgi sūryacandramasau vidhṛtau tiṣṭhataḥ |
content.text: O Gargi, by the command of this Imperishable, the sun and the moon are held in their positions.
This Atman is Brahman (Ayam Atma Brahma). The third of the four great sayings (Mahavakyas) — the individual soul is identical with the universal consciousness.
The mind alone is the cause of bondage and liberation for human beings.
